config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan batas yang jelas dan adil, bukan dengan menekan kebebasan pribadi secara berlebihan. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang bisa menjaga keseimbangan, bukan yang selalu menempatkan individu di bawah kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests ahead of individual rights as a general rule. From a United States perspective, that kind of claim makes me nervous because history shows how easily “the common good” can become an excuse for government overreach, censorship, or discrimination against unpopular minorities. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, since public health, safety, and basic law enforcement sometimes require real limits. But the burden should stay on society to justify those limits, not on individuals to prove they deserve their rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, saya melihat alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau melegitimasi kebijakan yang tidak adil. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi dalam situasi tertentu seperti keselamatan publik atau kesehatan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang tertib, melainkan kekuasaan kolektif yang terlalu mudah menginjak orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From an American point of view, that framing has too much potential to justify abuse, because once you say the collective comes first, it becomes very easy for authorities to silence dissent or sacrifice minorities for convenience. I do think there are narrow cases where individual rights can be limited for public safety or public health, but those limits need to be specific, temporary, and tightly justified. In my view, a healthy society protects people precisely by refusing to treat their rights as optional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus berada di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai terlalu longgar, dan akibatnya bisa berupa pembungkaman kritik, ketidakadilan bagi minoritas, atau kebijakan yang merugikan warga biasa. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti darurat kesehatan atau keamanan publik, tetapi pembatasan hak harus benar-benar sempit, proporsional, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan dalih rutin. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru yang berani melindungi hak individu, karena dari situlah keadilan dan kepercayaan publik tumbuh.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, I am wary of any argument that puts the collective first, because it too easily becomes a cover for overreach, censorship, and mistreatment of unpopular people. That said, I do not believe rights are untouchable in every circumstance; public health and safety can justify narrow limits, but those limits should be temporary, specific, and heavily scrutinized. In my view, a strong society is one that protects individuals first and only restricts them when it can clearly prove that doing so is necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
